# Memact

Memact lets apps use helpful memory only after a user approves the app, scopes, activity categories, and data transparency.

## What Memact Is

Memact is the product name. Access, capture, schema, and memory are functions or layers, not separate brands.

Apps use Memact when they need user-approved context such as summaries, evidence snippets, activity categories, graph objects, or schema packets. Apps should not receive a blanket dump of a user's private memory graph.

## Key Pages

- https://www.memact.com/
- https://www.memact.com/learn/
- https://www.memact.com/llms.txt
- https://www.memact.com/connect
- https://www.memact.com/DataTransparency
- https://github.com/Memact
- https://github.com/Memact/Website

## User Concepts

- Users approve or cancel an app before it connects.
- Users should see Data Transparency beside the consent flow.
- Data Transparency should explain the actual captured fields, evidence cards, summaries, graph packets, retention, and revocation path.
- Categories and scopes are boundaries, not full disclosure.

## Developer Concepts

- Register an app.
- Choose the smallest scopes and activity categories needed.
- Put a Connect Memact button in the app.
- Link the matching Data Transparency page beside consent.
- Store the returned `connection_id` for that user.
- Keep the raw Memact API key in server environment config, such as `MEMACT_API_KEY` in `.env` locally and a secret manager in production. This is the private app key created in Memact Access and usually starts with `mka_`.
- App developers should not configure Supabase keys. Any public transport key used by Memact verification is Memact infrastructure and should be handled by the Memact SDK, generated snippet, or hosted endpoint.
- Verify `api_key + connection_id + required_scopes` before using Memact output.
- Use only the scopes and categories returned by verification.

## For AI Coding Agents

If a developer asks an agent to "use Memact", build the same integration a human developer would build:

- Add a Connect Memact action that sends the user to `https://www.memact.com/connect` with the app id, requested scopes, activity categories, and redirect URL.
- Add the matching Data Transparency link so the user can inspect actual captured fields, evidence, summaries, graph packets, retention, and revocation before and after consent.
- Handle the redirect from Memact and store the returned `connection_id` for that user and app.
- Keep the raw Memact API key in a server environment variable or secret manager. Do not ask users to paste it into UI, and do not place it in browser JavaScript, mobile bundles, public repos, logs, screenshots, or emails.
- Before reading Memact output, verify the API key, connection id, and required scopes server-side.
- Treat scopes and activity categories as hard limits. If access is missing, send the user back through consent instead of silently widening data use.

## Short Definition

Memact lets apps remember useful context from user-approved activity without giving them blanket access.
